item.title: Mass immigration is not the silver bullet economists think it is
item.description: As with the free-trade debate in recent years, consensus is shifting

item.title: Nato’s carnival of Trumpian caprice
item.description: Alliance summits have become an exercise in managing an erratic US president

item.title: Don’t underestimate Farage in this Very British Farce
item.description: The ‘establishment’ he is so fond of bashing must beware of playing into stereotype

item.title: Iran is seeing through Trump’s bluff
item.description: The US president finds himself stuck in a cul-de-sac of his own making

item.title: What’s the etiquette for a WhatsApp street group?
item.description: Democracy or dictatorship, these are the rules of engagement

item.title: Erika McEntarfer: ‘You really can just fire the BLS commissioner’
item.description: The former labour statistics chief on the fragility (and strength) of US economic data

item.title: How AI changes the rules of engagement for sports viewers
item.description: Spectators will see more opportunity to move from pricey subscriptions and rigid schedules to more personalised feeds

item.title: SK Hynix’s jumbo share sale a sign of overheated times
item.description: Perhaps it will be some comfort to those with longer time horizons that market anomalies don’t last for ever

item.title: Why AI could be a financial ‘sludge’ buster
item.description: Regulators want to use the technology in their bid to cut red tape

item.title: How to protect gardens and pots from heat shocks
item.description: Even brief temperature spikes can kill plants or stress roots — particularly if they are over-planted or in plastic containers

item.title: There is no grand plan on Iran
item.description: An over-reaching Tehran seems to be looking at America’s confused moves and wondering what it may be missing

item.title: Microsoft’s early AI lead has become a test of faith
item.description: The only certainty is that capital spending is going through the roof

item.title: Britain’s unsustainable mental health bill
item.description: Reducing spending on disability benefits is a major test for Andy Burnham

item.title: Business model in terminal decline? Try a round of golf
item.description: Versant Media hopes to get back in play with its purchase of simulator maker Full Swing

item.title: How much is AI hacking your emotions?
item.description: Disinformation and deepfakes are known risks but biased LLMs could also be used to influence public opinion

item.title: Burnham must resist the anti-tech tendency
item.description: It would be a crime to lose the UK’s envied tech and life science status within Europe

item.title: Devolution has no easy answers on ‘fairness’
item.description: The consensus backing more local power sharing rarely confronts fundamental trade-offs

item.title: China could be the US tech hedge
item.description: Diversification is not about finding a market without risk — it is about finding a market whose risks are different

item.title: Around the World Cup in seven days
item.description: What it’s like living inside the world’s biggest football tournament

item.title: Europe’s ‘China shock’ panic is ill-informed
item.description: EU industry is largely weathering the competition well

item.title: Why isn’t the Fed raising rates?
item.description: And more on the job market

item.title: The Iran global inflation crisis that stubbornly refuses to happen
item.description: Neither the war in the Gulf nor the Covid-Ukraine price spike has taken us back to the stagflationary 1970s

item.title: Private assets could work for retail investors — at the right price
item.description: Complexity, opacity and liquidity issues are surmountable, but there is a more important barrier: fees

item.title: Why AI financial advisers have a leg-up on their old-world rivals
item.description: This isn’t a simple case of fusty incumbents disrupted by novel technology

item.title: Who wants to tax a billionaire?
item.description: A gentle guide to a harsh debate
